abet: verb. To incite; to assist or encourage by aid or countenance in crime.
aby: verb. To pay the penalty for (something); to atone for, to make amends.
age: noun. The amount of time that some being has been alive, or that some thing has been in existence, as measured from its birth or origin
agee: adverb. Aside, on or to one side; awry; off from the straight line.
bag: noun. A soft container made out of cloth, paper, thin plastic, etc. and open at the top, used to hold food, commodities, and other goods.
bam: interjection. Representing a loud noise or heavy impact.
bat: noun. Any flying mammal of the order Chiroptera, usually small and nocturnal, insectivorous or frugivorous.
bate: verb. To reduce the force of something; to abate.
bay: noun. A body of water (especially the sea) contained by a concave shoreline.
beam: noun. Any large piece of timber or iron long in proportion to its thickness, and prepared for use.
beamy: adjective. Resembling a beam in size and weight; massy.
bee: noun. A flying insect, of the clade Anthophila within the hymenopteran superfamily Apoidea, known for its organised societies (though only
beet: noun. Beta vulgaris, a plant with a swollen root which is eaten or used to make sugar.
beg: verb. To request the help of someone, often in the form of money.
beget: verb. To produce or bring forth (a child); to be a parent of; to father or sire.
bema: noun. A platform from which speakers addressed an assembly.

beta: noun. The second letter of the Greek alphabet (Β, β), preceded by alpha (Α, α) and followed by gamma, (Γ, γ).

bye: noun. The position of a person or team in a tournament or competition who draws no opponent in a particular round so advances to the next
byte: noun. A short sequence of bits (binary digits) that can be operated on as a unit by a computer; the smallest usable machine word.
eat: verb. To ingest; to be ingested.
eta: noun. The seventh letter of the Modern Greek alphabet, the eighth in Old Greek.
eye: noun. An organ through which animals see (“perceive surroundings via light”).
gaby: noun. A stupid, foolish person; a simpleton; a dunce.
gam: noun. A person's leg, especially an attractive woman's leg.
gamb: noun. The leg or shank, typically of an animal, especially of a lion, on a coat of arms, crest, etc.
game: noun. A playful or competitive activity.
gamete: noun. A reproductive cell (sperm in males or eggs in females), having only half of a complete set of chromosomes.
gamey: adjective. Having the smell, taste and texture of game meat.
gate: noun. A doorlike structure outside a house.
gay: adjective. Homosexual: Only possessing sexual and/or romantic attraction towards people one perceives to be the same sex or gender as
gee: interjection. A general exclamation of surprise or frustration.
gem: noun. A precious stone, usually of substantial monetary value or prized for its beauty or shine.
geta: noun. A Japanese raised wooden clog, worn with traditional Japanese garments such as the kimono.
gybe: verb. To shift a fore-and-aft sail from one side of a sailing vessel to the other, while sailing before the wind.
gym: noun. Clipping of gymnasium; especially, a school gymnasium.
mae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Bo-Rukul.
mage: noun. A magician, wizard, sorcerer, witch, warlock or mystic.
mat: noun. A flat piece of coarse material used for wiping one’s feet, or as a decorative or protective floor covering.
mate: noun. A fellow, comrade, colleague, partner or someone with whom something is shared, e.g. shipmate, classmate.
may: verb. To be strong; to have power (over).

met: verb. simple past and past participle of meet
metage: noun. Measurement, usually by weight, especially of coal for trading purposes.
mete: verb. To dispense, measure in order to dispense, allot (especially punishment, reward etc.).
tab: noun. A small flap or strip of material attached to or inserted into something, for holding, manipulation, identification, opening etc.

tam: noun. Clipping of tam o'shanter, a type of cap.

tee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter T / t.

yam: noun. Any climbing vine of the genus Dioscorea in the Eastern and Western hemispheres, usually cultivated.
yet: adverb. Thus far; up to the present; up to some unspecified time.
abey: verb. To hold in abeyance; to suspend.
agy: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Alta.
ambe: noun. An old mechanical contrivance, ascribed to Hippocrates, for reducing dislocations of the shoulder.
beety: adjective. Resembling or characteristic of beets.
begay: verb. To make cheerful or merry.
begem: verb. To adorn (as if) with gems.
beme: noun. A mediaeval trumpet, usually used in hunting or warfare.
betag: verb. To furnish with a tag; deck with tags.
gambet: noun. Any bird of the obsolete genus Totanus, now principally in genus Tringa; a tattler.

yee: pronoun. You (the people being addressed); alternative spelling of ye.
